summary: Alfred has strong documentation with comprehensive README, 8 ADRs covering major architectural decisions, and good inline code documentation. Coverage of public APIs is solid with 162 of 208 public items documented (~78%). Main gaps: some complex high-LOC files (app.rs 7987 lines, bridge.rs 5198 lines) have minimal inline docs relative to complexity, and a few design patterns lack usage examples in core modules.

worst_undocumented.name: handle_colon_command
worst_undocumented.reason: Complex command parsing logic (1000+ lines) with minimal context docs

worst_undocumented.name: register_*_primitives (15 functions)
worst_undocumented.reason: FFI bridge functions map Lisp to Rust; each has doc comment but no usage examples

worst_undocumented.name: execute_char_find, execute_motion
worst_undocumented.reason: Complex vim motion resolution; docs explain 'what' but not 'why' design decisions

worst_undocumented.name: render_frame (and 8 helper functions)
worst_undocumented.reason: Rendering pipeline is documented but helper abstractions lack motivation

complexity_doc_gap.issue: Largest file in codebase; contains event loop, key conversion, and key dispatch. Only 12 doc comments total relative to 200+ public/internal functions. Cyclomatic complexity from operator-pending, text-object, and command parsing states is high.

Complex Rust-Lisp FFI bridge with 15 register_*_primitives functions, each with short doc comment but no usage examples or patterns. Lisp primitive signatures are not documented (users must read tests to understand).
Core state machine with 40+ public functions. Comments explain implementation but lack high-level state machine diagrams or invariant documentation.
Well-documented module header and public functions. 162 doc comments indicate good coverage.

sections: Usage (basic workflow, editing commands, navigation, visual mode, text objects, marks/macros, command line)
Plugins (built-in plugins, creating plugins, available primitives)
missing_sections: Contributing guidelines (beyond code style in Makefile)
readme_assessment.notes: README is comprehensive, accurate, and well-organized. Installation instructions are clear and tested (make install, dev_install paths work). Usage section covers all major commands with examples. Plugin creation example is concrete and runnable. Architecture section explains crate boundaries and functional-core/imperative-shell pattern well.

adrs.title: ADR-001: Adopt Existing Lisp
adrs.impact: Foundational: chose rust_lisp over custom build

adrs.title: ADR-002: Plugin-First Architecture
adrs.impact: Core: all features > primitives are plugins

adrs.title: ADR-003: Single-Process Synchronous
adrs.impact: Performance: no threading, event loop driven

adrs.title: ADR-008: Folder Browser Architecture
adrs.impact: Feature: core-only approach, Lisp plugin keybindings

adr_assessment.notes: All ADRs follow standard format (Context, Decision, Alternatives, Consequences). Alternatives sections are detailed with evaluation of trade-offs. ADRs are current and reflect actual implementation. ADR-008 is the most recent (6 days old).

changelog_assessment.recommendation: Create CHANGELOG.md following Keep-a-Changelog format to track features, fixes, and breaking changes per release.

steps: make install (3-4 min: builds release, installs binary, updates PATH)
onboarding_assessment.dev_deps_notes: Makefile clearly lists: rustup, Rust stable, Docker (for E2E). dev_install includes rustfmt, clippy, pre-commit hooks.
onboarding_assessment.test_docs_notes: Makefile provides: make test (unit), make e2e (pexpect), make lint (clippy), make format (rustfmt). E2E tests are Docker-based; instructions are clear.
env_vars_list: CARGO_TERM_COLOR (set in CI for colored output)

issues.issue: README does not mention that pre-commit hooks are optional, or how to bypass them for WIP commits
E2E tests require Docker; this could be mentioned upfront in Development section

recommendations.title: Add inline state machine diagram to editor_state.rs
recommendations.description: Document the mode transitions (normal -> insert, visual mode selection start/end, operator-pending flow) with ASCII diagrams or links to external docs. Helps contributors understand the control flow.
recommendations.impact: Reduces onboarding time for mode-related changes; currently new contributors must trace through execute_key logic

recommendations.title: Create bridge.rs usage guide with Lisp primitive examples
recommendations.description: In docs/feature/lisp-bridge/, add examples of each Lisp primitive family (buffer, cursor, keymap, theme, etc.) with Rust implementation patterns. Currently users must read tests or source.
recommendations.impact: Enables plugin developers to extend Alfred Lisp API faster without reading FFI code

recommendations.title: Add CHANGELOG.md with Keep-a-Changelog format
recommendations.description: Track releases, features, fixes, breaking changes. Can be auto-generated from conventional commit messages (which are already used).
recommendations.impact: Users can understand what changed between versions; improves project maturity perception

recommendations.title: Document app.rs key dispatch logic with sequence diagrams
recommendations.description: The event loop and key handling in app.rs (7987 lines) uses PendingChar, OperatorPending, TextObject states. Add docs explaining state transitions and the operator-motion composition pattern.
recommendations.impact: Reduces defects in vim command implementation; makes it easier to add new operators or motions

recommendations.title: Add API documentation generation to CI
recommendations.description: Run cargo doc in CI and publish to docs/ or GitHub Pages. Currently no auto-generated API docs, only handwritten.
recommendations.impact: Users can browse API without building locally; improves discoverability of public functions
